# BeMap JS API — LLM integration guide

> This file follows the emerging `llms.txt` convention
> (https://llmstxt.org/) — a single-file source of truth that AI coding
> assistants (Claude, Cursor, Copilot, ChatGPT, …) can ingest to write
> correct, idiomatic BeMap code in one shot.

## What this library is

BeMap JS API is BeNomad's commercial JavaScript SDK for embedding
**maps + geocoding + routing + EV** features in web apps. It wraps three
map engines (OpenLayers v10, Leaflet 1.9, MapLibre GL v5) behind one
uniform API. Same code, three engines.

- License: commercial — see [LICENSE.md](LICENSE.md)
- Distribution: pre-built bundle in `dist/` — no Node runtime required at
  consumer site.
- Bundle size: ~308 KB (`dist/bemap-js-api.js`), ~109 KB minified
  (`dist/bemap-js-api.min.js`).
- Versions: v1.x = WMS only · v2.x = WMS + BeNomad Tiles (vector PMTiles)
  + Service Worker tile cache + MapLibre GL.

## Cardinal rules for AI assistants

1. **Never invent API methods.** If you can't find a method on
   `bemap.Map`, `bemap.LeafletMap`, `bemap.Ol3Map`, `bemap.MapLibreMap`,
   `bemap.Marker`, `bemap.Polyline`, `bemap.Polygon`, `bemap.Circle`,
   `bemap.Context`, `bemap.Coordinate`, `bemap.Color`, `bemap.LineStyle`
   etc., the user is wrong — clarify or refuse rather than fabricate.
   The full list is in `dist/doc/index.html` (JSDoc).
2. **Never break retrocompatibility.** BFleet, EVMove5, zone-optimizer
   and BeMap-Doc consume this library. The signatures in
   `src-test/test-retro-*.js` are contracts. 1004 specs gate every merge.
3. **The map class IS the switch** between WMS and BeNomad Tiles:
   - `bemap.LeafletMap(ctx, target)` → WMS, always.
   - `bemap.Ol3Map(ctx, target)` → WMS, always.
   - `bemap.MapLibreMap(ctx, target)` → BeNomad Tiles when
     `ctx.tilesHost` is set; falls back to a minimal background otherwise.
4. **`bemap.BemapLayer({ name: 'background' })` adapts to the engine.**
   On Leaflet/OL it produces a WMS layer. On MapLibre with `tilesHost`
   set, it produces the BeNomad PMTiles source. Same call, different
   engine — do NOT instantiate engine-specific layer classes.
5. **The Service Worker `dist/bemap-sw-tiles.js` must sit at the site
   root.** The library auto-registers it from `/bemap-sw-tiles.js`. Do
   not put it under `dist/` in the customer's deployment.

## Minimal working example (BeNomad Tiles path)

```html
<!doctype html>
<html><head>
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="/dist/maplibre-gl.css">
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="/dist/bemap-js-api.css">
  <script src="/dist/maplibre-gl.js"></script>
  <script src="/dist/pmtiles.js"></script>
  <script src="/dist/bemap-js-api.js"></script>
</head>
<body>
  <div id="map" style="position:absolute;inset:0;background:#d4ecf5"></div>
  <script>
    var ctx = new bemap.Context({
      host: 'bemap.benomad.com', secure: true,
      login: 'your-login', password: 'your-password',
      tilesHost: 'mptiles-api-beta.benomad.net'
    });
    var map = new bemap.MapLibreMap(ctx, 'map');
    map.move(2.35, 48.85, 12);

    // Add a marker. SAME API on all three engines.
    map.addMarker(new bemap.Marker(
      new bemap.Coordinate(2.35, 48.85),
      { icon: new bemap.Icon({ src: '/marker.svg', anchorX: 0.5, anchorY: 1,
                               anchorXUnits: 'fraction', anchorYUnits: 'fraction' }) }
    ));
  </script>
</body></html>
```

## Public API surface (the things AI assistants may call)

### Classes

- `bemap.Context({ host, secure, login, password, authInPost, geoserver?, tilesHost?, tilesFile?, tilesStyle?, glyphsUrl? })`
  - `tilesHost` must be paired with `host` per env (beta/preprod/prod). MapLibre map = which PMTiles map: `tilesFile → geoserver → 'default'` (server-resolved); `glyphsUrl` overrides the font host (default: fonts bundled in `dist/fonts/`, auto-detected).
- `bemap.LeafletMap(ctx, target, options?)` — Leaflet engine
- `bemap.Ol3Map(ctx, target, options?)` / `bemap.OlMap` — OpenLayers v10 engine
- `bemap.MapLibreMap(ctx, target, options?)` — MapLibre v5 engine (BeNomad Tiles)
- `bemap.BemapLayer({ name, styles? })` — engine-adaptive background layer
- `bemap.VectorLayer`, `bemap.ClusterLayer`, `bemap.RasterLayer`, `bemap.WmsLayer`
- `bemap.Marker(coordinate, options?)`, `bemap.Popup(coordinate, html|element)`
- `bemap.Polyline(coordinates, options?)`, `bemap.Polygon(coordinates, options?)`
- `bemap.Circle(coordinate, radiusMetres, options?)`
- `bemap.Coordinate(lon, lat)`, `bemap.BoundingBox(sw, ne)`
- `bemap.Color(r, g, b, a?)`, `bemap.Icon({ src, anchor*, ...})`
- `bemap.LineStyle`, `bemap.PolygonStyle`, `bemap.CircleStyle`,
  `bemap.TextStyle`, `bemap.ClusterStyle`
- `bemap.MapEvent` (has `.getCoordinate()`, `.bemapObject`)
- `bemap.Listener`
- `bemap.Error(code, status?, message?, context?)` — codes:
  `UNAUTHORIZED`, `FORBIDDEN`, `RATE_LIMITED`, `OFFLINE`,
  `STYLE_LOAD_FAILED`, `TILE_LOAD_FAILED`, `CACHE_HOST_CONFLICT`,
  `NETWORK`

### Map methods (work on every engine unless noted)

- Camera: `.move(lon,lat,zoom)`, `.flyTo(lon,lat,zoom)`,
  `.easeTo({...})`, `.jumpTo({...})`, `.moveToBoundingBox(bbox)`,
  `.moveToLayerData(layerName)`, `.zoom(level)`,
  `.getZoom()`, `.getCenter()`, `.getBoundingBox()`,
  `.rotation(deg)`, `.getRotation()`, `.refresh()`
- Camera (MapLibre + OL): `.setBearing(deg)`, `.getBearing()`,
  `.setPitch(deg)`, `.getPitch()`
- Camera (MapLibre only): `.setProjection('globe'|'mercator')`,
  `.spinGlobe({speed})`, `.stopSpinGlobe()`
- Layers: `.addLayer(layer)`, `.removeLayer(layer)`,
  `.clearLayer(layer)`, `.getLayerByName(name)`, `.refreshLayer(name)`,
  `.visibleLayer(name, bool)`, `.switchBackgroundLayer(name)`,
  `.defaultLayers(options?)`, `.zIndexLayer(layer, n)` (OL+LF only)
- Layers (MapLibre only): `.addRasterLayer(...)`,
  `.setStyle(nameOrJson)` (re-registers all overlays via the catalogue),
  `.setTerrain(opts)`, `.removeTerrain()`, `.add3DBuildings(opts)`,
  `.remove3DBuildings()`, `.setSky(opts)`, `.setLight(opts)`
- Markers: `.addMarker(marker)`, `.removeMarker(marker)`,
  `.onMarker(...)`, `.draggableMarker(marker, bool)`
- Polylines/polygons/circles: `.addPolyline(p)`, `.removePolyline(p)`,
  `.addPolygon(p)`, `.removePolygon(p)`, `.addCircle(c)`,
  `.addPopup(p)`, `.removePopup(p)`,
  `.draggablePolyline(p, bool)`
- Events: `.on(eventType, fn)`, `.off(eventType, fn)`
- Drag: `.getDragPan()`, `.setDragPan(bool)`, `.isDragPan()`
- Clustering: `.addClusterPoints(...)`
- Heatmap (MapLibre only): `.addHeatmap(data, opts)`,
  `.removeHeatmap(id)`
- Sources (MapLibre only): `.addGeoJsonSource(...)`, `.addImage(...)`,
  `.queryRenderedFeatures(...)`
- Animations (MapLibre only): `.animateAlongRoute(...)`,
  `.animateLine(...)`, `.animateCameraOrbit(...)`, `.animatePulse(...)`
- Cache (MapLibre + tilesHost): `.enableBrowserCache()`,
  `.disableBrowserCache()`, `.getBrowserCacheStats()`,
  `.clearBrowserCache()`, `map.on('cache:stats', cb)`
- Auth introspection (MapLibre + tilesHost): `.getToken()`,
  `.isTokenValid()`, `.refreshToken()`
- Escape hatch: `.native` — the underlying engine instance
  (`L.Map`, `ol.Map`, `maplibregl.Map`). Use sparingly; prefer the
  cross-engine API.

### Event types (`bemap.Map.EventType`)

`CLICK`, `MOVEEND`, `ZOOMEND`, `POINTERUP`, `POINTERDOWN`,
`POINTERDRAG`, `POINTERMOVE`, `SINGLECLICK`, `POSTRENDER`,
`PROPERTYCHANGE`, `CHANGE`, `CHANGE:SIZE`, `CHANGE:VIEW`.
The library translates these to the engine's native names internally.

### Default layer names (`bemap.Map.DEFAULT_LAYER`)

`BACKGROUND`, `MARKER`, `ROUTE`, `POLYLINE`. `defaultLayers()` creates
all four. Idempotent — safe to call twice.

### Services (v2 API)

Use these directly via `new bemap.GeocoderV2(ctx)` etc., or the v1
classes (`bemap.Geocoder`, `bemap.Autocomplete`, …) for retrocompat.

- `bemap.GeocoderV2(ctx)` — `.geocode({ query, lang?, limit? }) → Promise`
- `bemap.ReverseGeocoderV2(ctx)` — `.reverse({ lon, lat, lang? }) → Promise`
- `bemap.AutocompleteV2(ctx)` — `.suggest({ query, position?, lang? }) → Promise`
- `bemap.GeoAutocompleteV2(ctx)` — `.suggest({ query, lang? }) → Promise`
- `bemap.RoutingV2(ctx)` — `.route({ origin, destination, waypoints?, profile? }) → Promise`
- `bemap.IsochroneV2(ctx)` — `.isochrone({ lon, lat, durations|distances }) → Promise`
- `bemap.TraceRouteV2(ctx)` — `.trace({ coordinates, ... }) → Promise`
- `bemap.NearPoiV2(ctx)` — `.search({ lon, lat, radius, category? }) → Promise`
- `bemap.ChargingStationsV2(ctx)` — `.search(...) → Promise`
- `bemap.EvSmartRoutingV2(ctx)` — `.route(...) → Promise`
- `bemap.EvVehiclesV2(ctx)` — `.list() → Promise`
- `bemap.ChargingTimeV2(ctx)` — `.compute(...) → Promise`
- `bemap.EvReachableAreaV2(ctx)` — `.compute(...) → Promise`

## Anti-patterns AI assistants should refuse / rewrite

- **`new L.Map(...)` or `new maplibregl.Map(...)` directly** — defeats the
  whole point. Use `bemap.LeafletMap` / `bemap.MapLibreMap` and the
  retrocompat layer is preserved.
- **`map.native.flyTo(...)` reaching into the engine** — `.flyTo` exists
  on the bemap wrapper now. Use the wrapper.
- **Patching `window.fetch` to inject tokens** — v2 uses MapLibre's
  `transformRequest`. Never recommend the fetch patch.
- **Hardcoding `mptiles-api-beta.benomad.net` in customer code** — that's the
  default. Customer code should rely on `ctx.tilesHost` (let
  RuntimeConfig handle it) or pass their own host explicitly.
- **Manual `serviceWorker.register('/bemap-sw-tiles.js')`** — the library
  auto-registers it. Don't duplicate.
- **`map.setStyle(json)` without re-adding overlays** — on raw MapLibre
  this wipes user-added layers. `bemap.MapLibreMap.setStyle()`
  auto-replays from an internal overlay catalogue. Always use the
  wrapper.
- **Confusing `bemap.Color(r,g,b)` and `bemap.Color(r,g,b,a)`** — alpha
  is OPTIONAL (defaults to 1), but if you pass it must be 0–1 (not 0–255).

## Examples / tutorials shipped in the zip

- `examples/index.html` — full interactive dashboard. Open it directly
  in a browser. Sidebar covers every public method with a runnable demo.
- `docs/browser-cache.md` — Service Worker tile cache setup +
  verification + the six diagnostic console logs the library emits.
- `docs/migration-wms-to-tiles.md` — upgrade an existing v1 app to v2
  (one Context flag + class swap).
- `docs/style-customisation.md` — custom MapLibre styles via
  `opts.style`, registry resolution, bilingual labels.
- `docs/attributions-widget.md` — the BeNomad attribution widget.
- `docs/services-v2/` — per-service guides (geocoding, routing, POI, EV).
- `dist/doc/index.html` — JSDoc API reference (every public class with
  signatures + parameter types).

## Build pipeline (only relevant when an AI agent works on the source tree)

- `npx grunt scripts:dev` → emit `dist/bemap-js-api.js` (dev) +
  `dist/bemap-sw-tiles.js`.
- `npx grunt scripts:dist` → minify + copy vendored libs into `dist/`.
- `npx grunt package` → full clean rebuild + JSDoc + zip
  (`package/bemap-js-api.zip`).
- `npx grunt test` → Karma + ChromeHeadless (1004 specs, gating).

## Where to look first when asked something

- "How do I do X on a map?" → search `src/bemap-map.js` first for the
  abstract API, then the engine implementations in
  `src/bemap-{ol,leaflet,maplibre}/`.
- "What does the BeMap Context look like?" → `src/bemap.js` (search for
  `bemap.Context = function`).
- "What MapLibre option does the lib pass?" → `src/bemap-maplibre/
  bemap-maplibre-v5/bemap-map-maplibre.js`, search for
  `_nativeOpts = {`.
- "What's the public-method retrocompat contract?" →
  `src-test/test-retro-{bfleet,evmove5,bemap-doc}.js`. Tests there are
  immutable.
- "What service URLs does the library hit?" → `src/bemap.js`
  (`Context.getBaseUrl`), `src/bemap-services-v2/_base/
  bemap-service-v2.js`, individual service files.
- "How does authentication work?" →
  `src/bemap-maplibre/bemap-tiles-auth.js` for the BeNomad-Tiles JWT
  path; `src/bemap.js` (`Context.getBase64Auth` / `getUrlAuthParams`)
  for HTTP Basic against the WMS / services backend.

## Versioning

- Latest: **v2.0.0** (see [CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md)).
- BeNomad Tiles features (`MapLibreMap`, SW cache, JWT auth) require v2.
- v1 consumers (BFleet, EVMove5, zone-optimizer, BeMap-Doc) keep
  working on v2 unchanged — 1004 retrocompat specs gate every release.
